id: # SSOT for Amiga bootblock / link / file virus signatures.
# Schema:
#   id                     — stable numeric ID (never reused)
#   name                   — canonical virus name (xvs.library-compatible)
#   category               — bootvirus|linkvirus|filevirus|bootblock|trojan
#   pattern_hex            — byte signature, hex string (no spaces, uppercase).
#                            "PENDING" if signature has not yet been extracted
#                            from a reference source. Consumers skip matching
#                            for PENDING rows.
#   pattern_mask_hex       — byte-wise AND mask, same length as pattern_hex.
#                            "FF" bytes = exact match, "00" = don't care.
#                            "PENDING" when pattern is PENDING.
#   first_seen             — year first documented (approx; "?" if unknown)
#   source                 — primary reference (xvs.library / VHT-DK / public)
#   disinfect_strategy     — REPORT_ONLY (always — UFT does not kill viruses)
# Rules:
#   - Lines starting with # are comments, ignored by the generator.
#   - disinfect_strategy MUST be REPORT_ONLY for every row. UFT is a
#     forensic-preservation tool (see XCOPY_INTEGRATION_TODO § Anti-Features).
#   - Patterns start at bootblock byte 12 (after DOS type header + 4-byte
#     checksum + 4-byte root-block pointer) unless annotated otherwise.
#   - See docs/XCOPY_INTEGRATION_TODO.md T1 for the full plan.
# === Well-documented early Amiga bootblock viruses (pattern in progress) ===
# === Entries extracted from xvs.HISTORY (names only, signatures pending) ===
